findings.evidence: Changed proof lines 111-136 contain the integral/real-measure chain; Mathlib defines `measureT` as `(volume.withDensity ...).restrict (Set.Ioc (-1) 1)` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/SpecialFunctions/Trigonometric/Chebyshev/Orthogonality.lean:49`.
findings.fix: Unfold `Polynomial.Chebyshev.measureT` and derive the result from `self_mem_ae_restrict` or `ae_restrict_of_forall_mem` for `Set.Ioc (-1) 1`, followed by the elementary inclusion `Ioc (-1) 1 ⊆ Icc (-1) 1`.
findings.issue: `ae_mem_Icc_measureT` proves support in `[-1,1]` via `ae_iff`, `Measure.real`, `integral_indicator_one`, `integral_measureT`, and `intervalIntegral.integral_congr_ae_restrict`, obscuring that this is just the support of a restricted measure.

summary: The new consumer lemmas are short, but the support lemma proves a definitional property of `measureT` through a brittle integral/real-measure calculation. Replace that proof with one using the `restrict` definition of `measureT` so the proof exposes why it works.
